card.intuition: The LCA of two nodes p and q is the deepest node that has both as descendants — once the recursion finds p or q, it reports upward; the first node that gets reports from both sides is the LCA.
card.analogy: Two cousins tracing their family tree — they each walk up their lineage; the first common ancestor they reach (the deepest one) is their LCA.
card.time_complexity: O(n) naive DFS; O(log n) per query with binary lifting preprocessing in O(n log n)
card.space_complexity: O(n) for DFS stack; O(n log n) for binary lifting table
when_to_use: Finding the nearest common ancestor of two nodes in a tree
Computing distances between two nodes: dist(u,v) = depth(u) + depth(v) - 2*depth(LCA(u,v))
Path queries on trees — the path from u to v goes through LCA(u,v)
Repeated LCA queries on the same tree (use binary lifting or Euler tour + RMQ)
gotchas: If one node is an ancestor of the other, that ancestor IS the LCA — the recursive solution handles this naturally by returning the found node immediately
The naive approach requires the tree to be rooted — an unrooted tree needs a root choice first
Binary lifting requires O(n log n) preprocessing but answers each query in O(log n) — worth it for many queries

exercise.prompt: Find the LCA using a single post-order DFS. Return the LCA node.
exercise.solution:
return root
return left if left else right
hints: When both left and right are non-None, the current node is the LCA — it has one target on each side
When only one is non-None, that subtree contains both targets (or found one) — propagate it up
exercise.test_code:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self,v,l=None,r=None): self.val=v;self.left=l;self.right=r

n3=TreeNode(3); n5=TreeNode(5); n1=TreeNode(1)
n6=TreeNode(6); n2=TreeNode(2,TreeNode(7),TreeNode(4)); n0=TreeNode(0); n8=TreeNode(8)
n5.left=n6; n5.right=n2; n1.left=n0; n1.right=n8
n3.left=n5; n3.right=n1

assert lca(n3,n5,n1).val == 3, 'LCA(5,1) = 3'
assert lca(n3,n5,n4:=n2.right).val == 5, 'LCA(5,4) = 5  (5 is ancestor of 4)'
assert lca(n3,n6,n4).val == 5, 'LCA(6,4) = 5'
print('PASS')
